summary: PRD V2 battery round 2 — fresh DEEP angles (balance-math tie-out, build-plan attack, narrative deep-read, determinism red-team, dropped-content diff, fidelity-subset) + completeness-critic; dedups vs round-1 known findings; reports NEW actionable + a dryness verdict
logs: round2: 41 findings, 31 NEW actionable, verifying 9 new crit/high

new_blocking: B4 — Rung-meter back-solve uses a SINGLE scalar RUNG_FLOOR_MIN=30 (§4.1.1 L3396), so meter-fill = threshold/rate = ~30 min FLAT for every T0 rung, but §4.8.1 (L4347-4360) assigns escalating wall-clocks 30/30/30/35/40/45/55. The meter therefore clears R4-R7 5-25 min early. Two horns, both real: (a) if the meter is the binding 'gate to LEAVE,' T0 lifetime koku = 30·500 = 15.0K, NOT the 21.5K spine §4.0 (L3234-3235) derives from the wall-clocks, breaking the '~21K produced / ~18-19K held NET' tie-out and the E2-at-R6 affordability chain; (b) if story flags hold R4-R7 to 35-55 min, the meter is non-binding for 4 of 7 T0 rungs (and most V/G rungs), falsifying §4.1.1's 'in lockstep with the §4.8.1 koku column' (L3393) and 'land together' (L3401-3402) and making the §6.6 koku-lockstep verifier failing-or-vacuous. FIX: back-solve each rung's threshold against THAT rung's wall-clock (per-rung floor array), not one scalar — refutes round-1's praised 'rung-meter ≡ time-floor off one constant' positive. needs-human.

B5 — Cross-pillar-combo gate-EXCLUSION is unrepresentable + the PRD self-contradicts on where a combo writes. §6.4 (L5775-5777) stores one influence[pillar].value per pillar with NO deed-vs-combo provenance split and no deed ledger (counts is explicitly not one, L5786), yet §4.3.1 rule 3 (L3767-3769) + gateProfile (L5817) demand combo-ip be excluded from the hybrid gate — no field exists to subtract it; rule 2 also raises highWater, so combo-ip re-enters via the seasonal 30% strand. Compounded by a Model-A-vs-B contradiction: §1.6.1/§2.16/§4.3.1 (L508/L2055-2059/L3766) say a combo emits into BOTH paired pillars, but §6.5 content/influence.ts (L5884), the §6.6 verifier (L5908) and the §6.6.1 trade proof (L5959) say combos land on a THIRD pillar (Name). Both cannot hold; under Model-A the M5 phase-7 (L6808) / M6 phase-5 (L6870) 'gate-excluded' verifier assertions have no structural hook. FIX: pick the model; if Model-A, add an additive deed-only gateEligibleValue accumulator combos don't write + decide combo→highWater/seasonal/display semantics. needs-human.

B6 — Day-keyed world-state schema contradiction (NEW, absent from R1 + the audit ledger). §2.2 (L1212-1216) says 'only the day index and tick persist' (season/year DERIVED from an absolute day), but §6.4 (L5763) stores clock:{tick,day,season,year} and §2.19 (L2229) persists all four — and deriveDayKeyed (§6.7.1 L6008) is keyed only on (seed,channel,day). Under the absolute-day model season=floor((day-1)/30)%4 and year=floor((day-1)/120) are pure functions of day (§4 L3214), so storing them violates §6.4's OWN cardinal 'store only non-derivable' rule (L5751) and lets stored season/year desync after any migration/import/edit. Separately, deriving 'lunar phase' from a per-day splitmix64 hash teleports the moon between phases instead of waxing/waning over a continuous ~29.5-day cycle (and a 30-day reset can never represent 29.5 d). FIX: pin day = absolute monotonic count, make season/year derived selectors (don't store), define lunar = f(absoluteDay mod LUNAR_PERIOD) not an RNG roll. Schema-affecting (touches §6.4/§2.19 + M0 build); needs-human (lunar-as-ephemeris-vs-roll is a world-sim design call to ADR).

B7 — Multi-tab concurrent play silently clobbers a save — a 'silent loss' vector DISTINCT from B3 (merged from two round-2 lenses). Grep across prd.md/feedback/decisions finds ZERO Web Locks (navigator.locks), BroadcastChannel, storage-event, single-instance, mutex, or leader-election anywhere. The newest-wins selector (§6.8.1 L6089-6098) is a scalar per-context saveCounter + savedAt-ms tiebreaker that arbitrates only across BACKENDS, not across live TABS. IndexedDB+localStorage are same-origin-shared, so two tabs both load counter=N, each increments in-memory and writes divergent state; last-writer-wins destroys the other tab's session, and the still-running tab re-clobbers on its next autosave. A scalar monotonic-per-context counter provably cannot detect concurrent divergence (needs a vector clock / writer-id / lock). This is a routine scenario (restore-tabs, reopen bookmark) directly violating the save layer's headline 'no silent loss,' AMPLIFIED by FU23's 'leave it running in a tab' design (L948/L1318-1321). FIX: Web Locks single-writer election (2nd tab read-only), or per-tab instanceId + storage-event conflict detection → recovery prompt; itch cross-origin-iframe partitioning constrains these APIs. needs-human (policy + 2nd-tab UX, ADR-worthy parallel to D-013).

new_human_questions.q: Double-resurrection onto Oyuki: inside the single Origin arc (O0→O5, all in T2), the same grieving mother gets back TWO separately-presumed-dead loved ones as separate beats — her son (MC) at O1 and her husband Jinpachi at O4 (which 'opens LATE'). New evidence: the Q11 debt→resurrection reframe CREATED this (it swapped Kuranosuke's debt-bondage for a clean resurrection); the father's disappearance has NO authored cause anywhere; and §3.6.2 O4 ('grieved as away/LOST') already disagrees with §5 T2.5 L5163/5170 ('presumed him dead / back-from-the-dead'). Resolve toward O4's own 'away, known-alive' wording (near-zero rewrite, single homecoming payoff), or keep both returns with an on-screen reckoning + a differentiated third reveal-shape? Sits inside the existing [FLAG-HUMAN] #2 / Q11.

Gender-drift 'fair clue' (§1.4 L204-206) is both internally contradictory and mis-labelled 'fair.' The drift's job is to license a young MAN being accepted as returned-Tama, yet the two named anchors of the legend are the very people who knew girl-Tama: Sayo ('the living heart of the legend,' ~16, spontaneously recognizes him at V0) and 'a household that flinches at her name' (Tama's own family) — the drift would have to skip exactly the witnesses who carry it. And the §1.9 clue list plants NO forward girl-signal: the only gender datum pre-G6 is the legend's wrong 'boy,' which points at the FALSE reading, so the clue only works backward at the reveal — undercutting the no-character-creator rationale. Recast the 'boy' drift as a MOTIVATED family cover-story (burying the near-sale shame) so it becomes forward-solvable, decouple Sayo's certainty, or soften §1.4's 'fair, re-readable' claim? ADR-level (keystone mystery).

M5 pre-split (Q8b, sibling to Q8): M5 — the milestone that declares v1 content-complete — is the largest/densest slice (8 phases) with NO up-front split, introducing THREE net-new subsystems cold (the sekisho travel-standing gating PRIMITIVE, the whole Origin faction with its double STORY-AND-PILLAR gate + always-≥1-ungated-beat invariant + missable/conditional epilogue, and the cross-pillar combos) plus a wide content surface. Pre-split it at the G3→G4 / Origin-open seam the way combat was pre-split M2a/M2b? NOTE: mark the round-2 'combo is foundational-to-the-end-gate' framing REFUTED in the ledger — §4.3.1 rule 3 makes combos gate-EXCLUDED and the end-gate is satisfiable with zero combo-ip; the real risk is M5's BREADTH and lack of a pre-split, not the combo.

Save 'redundancy' is non-independent against its OWN named threat. §6.8 (L6031) frames 3-backend redundancy as defence where 'any SINGLE backend may be wiped or isolated,' but iframe storage PARTITIONING applies one partition key uniformly to IDB+localStorage+sessionStorage — against the named itch cross-origin-iframe threat they fail together, not independently. Two compounding facts: sessionStorage contributes ZERO cross-session durability (effectively 2 durable backends, same partition), and WebKit/Safari (a named M7 target) blocks/ephemeralizes 3rd-party iframe storage by default — an ephemeral write SUCCEEDS then silently evicts after the session, so Q44's 'couldn't save — export a backup' banner never fires and §2.19(b)'s 'autosaves transparently' is silently false on itch-in-Safari, leaving the manual base64 export as the only real save. And the keystone is built-full-at-M0 but validated only at M7. Need: Storage Access API gesture / top-level-window fallback / treat base64 export as primary on hostile embeds / a write-then-read-back canary so eviction trips the banner / pull a LOCAL cross-origin-iframe partition test forward to M0-M1?

G2 spine event-log says 'Tahei…?' aloud to EVERY player (§3.6 L2924, a spine-guaranteed rung beat), contradicting the design that the true name is reclaimed only at the missable O5 Origin capstone (§1.4/Q5/D-036) and the T4.5 epilogue that withholds 'Tahei' from skip-Origin players. 'Reclaiming' is missable but 'learning the name' is now guaranteed. Blur/obscure the name on the G2 spine line (reserve the spoken name for the Origin track), or revise §1.4/T4.5 so the name is heard-but-unclaimed for all and the epilogue distinction is acceptance-not-knowledge?

Three neglected-upstream-embankment floods (the exact same mechanism) anchor the MC's near-death origin (§1.9 L779), the estate's founding debt (Sadamune's failed flood-venture, §1.11 L849), and the house's root-sin drowning of Kuzuhara (§3.6 G4 / §5 T2.2 L5052). The PRD forecloses an MC↔Kuzuhara personal tie (§5 flag #4) but never addresses that the MC was nearly killed by the very negligence the house is built on. Either connect them (powerful dramatic irony, but 'no personal tie' must be rewritten) or differentiate the MC's disaster's cause (rockfall-dammed gorge / landslide / storm-surge) so it isn't a third neglected embankment and the coincidence dissolves?

The M6 fun-proxy GATE (phase 4, L6869) promotes 'no dead-time' and 'reward cadence' to hard build-fail checks, but BOTH owning docs ship those thresholds as literal blanks ('> ~N s', 'every ~X min'; fun-factor.md §8 L313 + qa-playtesting.md §3 defer pinning to the M3 pass), and the dead-time detector ('no meaningful action AND no reward incoming') has no headless state-predicate. Pinning only after exploratory M3 play makes the gate self-fulfilling (catches regressions below the M3 baseline, never an already-boring baseline). Commit concrete v0 tunable numbers now, or scope the M6 gate to ONLY the four quantified proxies (first-5-min / visible-next-goal / ≥30-min / 70-30 / win-rate) and keep no-dead-time + reward-cadence report-only until pinned — and define the 'reward-incoming' predicate?

The headless auto-player ('the bot') is the single instrument behind EVERY pacing/fun acceptance check (≥30-min undershoot-fail, tier floors, 70/30, win-rate bands, fun proxies), yet its policy is one sentence ('pick the best legal action: highest-value available deed'). For a four-pillar HYBRID-breadth-gate game with trade-≤⅓, a 0.04 per-event cap, and required-pillar drift, 'highest-value deed' is ill-defined and an optimal bot must deliberately NOT over-specialize or it fails the breadth wall — i.e. the floor-validating bot must itself solve the game's central tension. A weak greedy bot makes a genuinely too-fast rung 'pass' (slow only because the bot is dumb). Specify the value function, the two bot variants, and a guard that the optimal bot saturates each rung's eligible-activity RATE — otherwise the undershoot gate measures the bot, not the game?

Name-pillar T2 deed-cap invariant violation (separate stale reference from B1). §4.2.1 lists Name T2 deed bases of 1.6K and 1.8K, but cap = 0.04·goodBand and §4.1's real Name T2 good band = 28K → cap = 1.12K, so BOTH bases EXCEED the cap the §6.6 verifier asserts. The Name cap column (T0 40 / T1 400 / T2 3.2K) back-implies good-bands 1K/10K/80K — none exist (T0=2, T1=3 pillars; Name first reveals at T2). Net: T2 Name now carries THREE inconsistent magnitudes — 28K (good band), 80K (cap-implied), 140K (B1 seasonal ref). Resolve via Q6: if Name is gated only at T2, delete the T0/T1 Name deed bases + orphan caps and set the T2 cap to 1.12K with bases tuned below it; if Name is a from-T0 display bar, author its T0/T1 good-bands in §4.1 and add Name to the deed-count sums — either way collapse the 28K/80K/140K trio to one value.

§4.2.2 phase-reconciliation prose contradicts its own worked tie-out. The prose (L3546-3552) says Phase-1 builds holdings so 'when Phase 2 opens the basis is already near band-top → a large first high-water jump, then diminishing,' but the worked table starts at basis 4% (f=0.20) and ramps to 100% — a basis building from scratch. If the basis were genuinely ~86% at Phase-2 open, ~93% of the seasonal 30% would post in ONE season (~34 min), contradicting the smooth 8-season back-credit the §4.8.1b pacing window assumes. Pick one model: gradual credit across Phase 2 (drop the 'near band-top → large first jump' prose), or near-top-at-open (redraw the table starting at ~80%+ basis and acknowledge the front-load)?

Q22 (human-signed) locks the T2 anti-slump as BOTH 'seasonal-reward rotation AND cross-pillar combos.' The combos half is fully built (formulas, pairs, magnitudes, 4 rules, verifier proofs); the seasonal-reward-rotation half is named as canon in 7 places (L508/869/2056/3454/3759/6776/6808) but NEVER specified — no rule, data shape, cadence, or player-facing behaviour, and the M6 Done-criterion just says '…and the seasonal-reward rotation lands' with zero criteria. §4.2.2's seasonal appraisal is a fixed high-water judgment, not a variety-rotation device. Add a §2.16/§4.x spec (what rewards rotate, the rule, the data shape, an M6 criterion), or rule that combos alone satisfy Q22 and annotate it?

Weapon archetype params are dead data vs the combat math. FU13/Q15 require per-weapon reach/targetCount + a signature ability and §4.6.9 tabulates them, but the only formula provided is strictly single-defender: §4.6.1 attackPower consumes weaponBase (not reach/targetCount), §4.6.3 resolves one defender with no targetCount loop or status math — so reach, targetCount, and all 10 signatures (armour-pierce, cleave-2, first-strike, crit-on-open, counter-on-evade, etc.) have no consumer; four weapons' 'cleave 2' merely duplicates their own baseline targetCount=2; and the bestiary's monkey-troop 'teaches multi-target' has no resolver. Worse, the table presented as 'byte-identical to items.ts' OMITS per-weapon weaponBase/attackProfile (the only damage param the engine reads) for 9 of 10 weapons. Add a multi-target/reach resolver + status math + a weaponBase column, or demote reach/targetCount/signatures to flavour and say so?

Renderer/DOM/reveal engine is entirely OUTSIDE npm run verify (tsc+eslint+prettier+vitest+verify-content+gen-docs all skip the DOM; __qa drives core intents and reads GameState, never renders src/ui/). The renderer holds the design's real concentrated complexity — the render(state,prevState) reconciliation ('kills the stale-UI bug class'), the unlock-predicate one-at-a-time reveal sequencing (the headline 'UI grows as you rise' feature), the K/M/B formatter, the mobile IA — yet all of it is validated only by the manual agent-eyeballed capture-game-states sweep at M6, which is NOT in the commit/release gate. A broken unlock predicate, out-of-order reveal, NO-UI-DUMP violation, or stale panel ships green. Add a render-layer test tier (jsdom/happy-dom or Playwright: unlockedSurfaces→DOM, reveal ordering, K/M/B formatting, large-textScale/narrow-viewport reflow snapshot), or is agent-eyeballed capture the accepted ceiling?

Accessibility was audited by no round-1 lens and owns no buildable gate-check. Every contrast claim ('--ink-soft passes WCAG AA on EVERY paper surface') is asserted but no ratio is ever computed, and the woodblock/ink palette is deliberately low-contrast — AA-on-every-surface is exactly the claim most likely to be false and is unverifiable as written. The a11y criteria have NO check in npm run verify (only the macron lint is a11y-adjacent), and the ARIA live-region 'polite, scoped to narration+milestone' on an idle game streaming log lines for hours is an untested screen-reader-flooding risk ('milestone' undefined). Add axe-core + a computed-contrast assertion over the actual palette tokens + a reduced-motion/large-scale reflow snapshot to the gate and pin the real ratios, or accept manual-M6-only?

Performance/memory — the keystone NFR for a ~60h 'leave-it-running' session — is deferred (R7/Q56) with no interim budget and nothing forcing one to ever exist (unlike pacing/fun, which become M6 gates). Uncovered hazards: the 'UI grows' engine accumulates DOM across ~9 nav tabs with no node ceiling; a rAF loop runs pure tick + full render reconciliation for hours; the 3-backend redundant save writes the whole growing envelope on a debounced+tick+visibilitychange cadence with no save-size cap (localStorage ~5MB) and only the 'couldn't save' banner as backstop; counts/flags/inventory/unlockedSurfaces grow unbounded. Set a provisional budget now (DOM-node ceiling, per-frame render-ms target, max save-envelope bytes) and make it an M6 gate, or accept ad-hoc M6/M7 smoke with no regression guard?

Integer-pow association order is unspecified, so two lint-conformant builds can derive different levels from the same save. §4.6.5 combatXpForLevel(L)=round(60·1.18^(L-1)) uses a fractional base; naive left-to-right repeated multiply and exponentiation-by-squaring differ in the last ULP, so round(...) can land on different integers at a boundary (level 12 vs 11). Since character.level is DERIVED from stored combatXp every load (HP/satietyMax/attribute-points/win-rate cascade off it), the SAME exported save yields different stats on different builds — defeating the 'exported save replays byte-identically across engines / is portable' claim across exactly the build-evolution the additive-schema model anticipates (the verifier only proves within-build replay). Pin ONE canonical association order in a shared core helper + a golden-vector test over the whole table, and treat any change as migration-triggering?

splitmix64 is LOCKED (§6.13#2) but seed/cursors are typed number (§6.4 L5761-5762) and JSON-serialized, with no BigInt anywhere. splitmix64's defining property is 64-bit unsigned integer arithmetic; JS number is an IEEE-754 double exact only to 2^53 and JSON has no 64-bit type — so the LOCKED algorithm can't be true reference splitmix64 while the seed stays a serializable number (either BigInt/u64-limb impl with a seed that can't round-trip as a JSON number above 2^53, or a number impl that is a non-reference lower-entropy generator). The stated rationale (the Math.pow ban makes the seed→sequence 'byte-identical') is a category error — Math.pow isn't used by splitmix64; 64-bit integer math is the real determinism requirement and is unspecified. Decide the impl (BigInt or two-u32 limbs), constrain the stored seed to the 53-bit safe range (serialize 64-bit state as string/limb-pair), add a golden-vector test, and remove the incorrect Math.pow rationale?

Co-location coincidence: Sawatari-juku is the only T2 post-town, and BOTH personal threads land there — Tahei's living family AND Otsuru (the runaway real Tama, 'alive and grown the whole decade in the post-town'). Every player brokers trade there from G2 (spine-guaranteed) yet the Otsuru truth is withheld until G6, with no in-fiction reason for the ~four-rung gap beyond 'too ashamed to return [to Asagiri].' Lampshade the co-location as thematic (the kaidō post-town is where the region's runaways/presumed-dead wash up) and plant a reason the MC doesn't cross Otsuru until G6 (she works out of sight / under another name / returns near G6), so the gap reads deliberate not gated-by-convenience?

Physician 'Ranpo' — the rational, kami-disbelieving debunker who 'names symptoms not visions' — unavoidably evokes Edogawa Ranpo (founder of Japanese detective fiction, a 1920s pen name ~150 years after the setting), an anachronistic wink that sits uneasily beside the §6.6 real-name discipline (and a pen name wouldn't trip the denylist). Intentional rangaku double-nod (蘭/Dutch-studies, fitting a rational physician) or an inadvertent slip? If the rangaku flavour is wanted, a less loaded name avoids the detective-fiction overtone; if intentional, annotate it so it isn't mistaken for a slip.

Estate × Name (celebrated meibutsu) combo softly bends the trade-≤⅓ INTENT. The meibutsu/silk is canonically the TRADE strand that 'lives entirely inside this capped strand' (§4.2.3 L3687), yet §4.3.1 routes its combo credit to the already-clamped Estate-general total (OUTSIDE the ⅓-capped trade sub-engine) plus ≈0.02·good-band into Name — so a trade-driven achievement also harvests Estate-general + Name grind-ip without counting against the trade ceiling. The HARD invariant (trade ratio ≤⅓, combo-ip gate-excluded) still holds, but the stated SPIRIT ('a pure-trade run maxes ~⅓ of one of four pillars') is dented. Confirm this bounded smoothing is intended, or exclude trade-sourced deeds from the Estate×Name combo?

Confirm the Q49/Q43 provenance: prd.md cites 'Q49' 6× (and the trivial-fix strikes it), but Q49 exists nowhere in the binding record and the human-feedback Q-sequence skips both Q43 and Q49. Was there a real Q43/Q49 decision that got dropped from prd_human_feedback.md (restore it), or are they stray ids (the strike is correct)?

new_strengths: Every milestone's Definition-of-Done reduces to a falsifiable headless __qa reach-state assertion (M0 phase-8 L6532/6550 through M6 DoD L6849-6862) — because reveals are predicate-driven off surfaces.ts and the core is deterministic under per-stream cursors, the build order is fine-grained, pass/fail (not vibes), and continuously green; the M2a/M2b fixed pre-split (auto-battler isolation-tested before the bestiary exists) is the model the M3/M5 scope questions should follow.

Clock-free deterministic core makes weather/save robust to DST / system-clock skew / timezone changes: the core never reads wall-clock, weather/lunar/festival are pure functions of (seed, day), and the newest-wins selector keys on the monotonic saveCounter with Date.now (savedAt) used ONLY as an equal-counter tiebreaker — the sole Date.now read is correctly quarantined to the save layer (residual exposure narrowed to the multi-tab tiebreaker, B7).

The thematic spine is tight and non-slop: the kamikakushi-as-grief master-lie (§1.10 L816-818) coherently unifies Tama, the drowned of Kuzuhara, and the MC's flood trauma as grief-coping rather than superstition-to-mock; and Jūbei's creed 'Talent is a story the lucky tell. You are not lucky. So you will work.' (§5 T0.2 L4629-4630) states the whole mediocre-start / no-hidden-edge thesis in-voice — both are the tonal benchmark the rest of the event-log copy should match (watch the parenthetical subtext-asides that drift lower).

The v1→v2 reshape is high-fidelity despite +4105/−1952 lines: the dropped-content sweep is essentially negative (every v1 cast name, all CJK/kanji terms, the 21-row §2 inventory, the ~10-weapon roster §2.10.1↔§4.6.9 tie, and the §4.8.1 T0 pacing table all transfer intact), and cross-ref integrity is clean (~70 distinct §X.Y refs resolve; every cited D-0xx ADR id resolves to decisions.md). The defects found are localized prose/provenance staleness + the Name-pillar tangle, not structural loss.

The Origin/Ties track (Q5/Q25/Q40/D-036) is implemented faithfully end-to-end: the O0→O5 ladder is fully tabulated with the name-reclaim placed at O5 as EARNED + MISSABLE, cleanly separated from the spine-guaranteed G6 Otsuru/Tama truth; the Q12 gift-vs-work guardrail is intact (returned memory = zero retroactive bonus; present-day pride/morale + trade-ties stay earned); cast renames are consistent and the D-009 ADR is properly annotated. (Caveat: the G2 'Tahei…?' spine line, surfaced above, is the one crack in the missable-name separation.)
synthesis.summary: VERDICT: NOT-DRY — round 2 was strongly productive, so the loop must continue. Two structural reasons the design lenses missed material: (1) round 1's positive/negative/persona lenses and even most round-2 design angles aim at the DESIGN spine and skip the non-functional / platform-reality / operational surface, which a single completeness pass mined heavily; (2) round 2's balance/determinism angles found genuinely new BREAKS the spine lenses didn't reach. Net new this round: FOUR new blocking defects of B1/B2/B3 caliber — B4 the rung-meter back-solve uses one scalar floor so the meter clears R4-R7 5-25 min early, which either collapses the 21.5K koku spine to ~15K or makes the meter cosmetic and the §6.6 lockstep verifier vacuous (this REFUTES a round-1 positive); B5 the cross-pillar-combo gate-exclusion is unrepresentable in the §6.4 schema AND the PRD self-contradicts on whether combos write both paired pillars (§4.3.1) or a third Name pillar (§6.5/§6.6), so the M5/M6 'gate-excluded' verifier has no hook; B6 a day-keyed world-clock schema contradiction (§2.2 says derive season/year, §6.4/§2.19 store them) plus lunar-as-RNG-hash; and B7 multi-tab concurrent play silently clobbers a save (no Web Locks/BroadcastChannel/storage-event anywhere) — a silent-loss vector distinct from B3 and amplified by the 'leave it running' design. Plus 5 apply-ready trivial fixes (the §6.6.1 guard still names the SUPERSEDED cast Mago/Naozane/Obaa Sato — round 1's fix missed this occurrence; the §4.2.2 footnote has season-1/season-2 inverted; audio-context/FU3-cursor/Q49 cleanups) and 20 ranked human questions spanning narrative design calls (double-resurrection onto Oyuki created by the Q11 reframe, the un-seeded gender-drift 'fair clue,' the G2 Tahei broadcast, triple-embankment-flood, co-location, Ranpo) and the entire NFR/platform surface (save-redundancy non-independence on itch-Safari, renderer/a11y/perf all outside the verify gate, the bot policy and fun-proxy thresholds underspecified, Q22's seasonal-reward-rotation half is vapor, integer-pow/splitmix64 determinism gaps). The design SPINE is approaching dryness (the angles are re-converging on the B1/B2/B3 + Q1-Q12 cluster), but the platform/NFR surface is freshly productive and has had only one focused pass. RECOMMENDATION: do NOT count this round dry; run round 3 with a dedicated NFR/platform-reality lens (save durability on the real itch host, multi-tab, perf budget, a11y verification, render-layer test coverage) and re-check the four new blocking math/schema defects before declaring two-consecutive-dry. Five strengths confirm the reshape is high-fidelity and the build spine is genuinely machine-verifiable; all blocking items are needs-human design calls, not typos. confirmed.title: Rung-meter thresholds are back-solved to the FLOOR value, not the escalating §4.8.1 wall-clocks — the meter clears 5–65 min before each rung ends, so it is NOT 'in lockstep with the koku column' and either breaks the 21.5K koku spine or makes the meter cosmetic
confirmed.detail: §4.1.1 defines thresholdForRung = RUNG_FLOOR_MIN · eligibleActivityRate, so fill-time = threshold/rate = RUNG_FLOOR_MIN for EVERY rung — the rate cancels. T0 uses RUNG_FLOOR_MIN=30 for all of R1–R7, so every T0 meter fills in exactly ~30 min. But §4.8.1 assigns escalating wall-clocks 30/30/30/35/40/45/55, i.e. R4–R7 are supposed to take 5/10/15/25 min LONGER than their meter fills. The gap is pervasive and worse at higher tiers (T1 floor 40 vs wall-clocks to 70 → gaps to 30 min; T2 floor 75 vs wall-clocks to 140 → gaps to 65 min). Consequence: if the meter is the binding gate (as §4.8.1's 'Meter+story gate to LEAVE it' column presents it, listing 'Estate Service ≥ ~14' beside a 55-min R7), a player leaves R4–R7 at ~30 min and T0 lifetime koku = 30·(25+35+40+60+80+110+150) = 15.0K, NOT the 21.5K spine — which then breaks the §4.0/§4.7.1 '~21K produced / ~18–19K held NET' tie-out and the E2-at-R6 affordability chain. If instead the story-flag side holds R4–R7 to 35–55 min, then the meter is non-binding for those rungs and §4.1.1's central claims — 'in lockstep with the §4.8.1 koku column' (line 3393) and 'the meter and the time floor are the same constraint... clear them at pace and the meter and the floor land together' (3401–3402) — are simply false for 4 of 7 T0 rungs (and most V/G rungs). The §6.6 'gate-monotonicity / koku-lockstep' verifier is therefore either failing or vacuous.
confirmed.fix: Back-solve each rung's threshold against THAT rung's §4.8.1 wall-clock floor, not a single per-tier RUNG_FLOOR_MIN — i.e. threshold(R7)=55·rate≈25, not 30·0.46≈14 — exactly as §4.1.1 already varies the floor across tiers (30/40/75) but fails to vary WITHIN a tier. Then re-state which side of the AND-gate sets the 35–55/70/140-min wall-clock, and make the verifier assert meter-fill-time == the §4.8.1 wall-clock per rung.
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED and genuinely new. The arithmetic reproduces exactly from the PRD. §4.1.1 (line 3396) defines thresholdForRung = RUNG_FLOOR_MIN(=30) · eligibleActivityRate, where RUNG_FLOOR_MIN is a SINGLE scalar (confirmed at 3396/3424/4475/4507 — no per-rung floor array exists). So meter-fill = threshold/rate = 30 min FLAT for every T0 rung; the printed thresholds 18/19/17/17/17/15/14 (table 3408-3416) are all exactly 30·rate, NOT back-solved to the §4.8.1 wall-clocks (those would give ~20/22/23/25 for R4-R7). §4.8.1 (line 4358) assigns escalating wall-clocks 30/30/30/35/40/45/55, so the meter clears R4-R7 by 5/10/15/25 min early. Both horns hold against the text with no escape reading: (1) if the meter is the binding 'gate to LEAVE it' (the §4.8.1 column header, AND-gate), the player leaves R4-R7 at 30 min and T0 koku = 30·(25+35+40+60+80+110+150) = 30·500 = 15.0K, a ~30% collapse of the 21.5K centrepiece spine that §4.0 (line 3235) explicitly derives from the escalating wall-clocks, breaking the '21K produced / 18-19K held NET' tie-out; (2) if instead the story flags hold R4-R7 to 35-55 min, the meter is non-binding/cosmetic for 4 of 7 rungs, directly falsifying §4.1.1's 'in lockstep with the §4.8.1 koku column' (3393) and 'same constraint... land together' (3401-3402) — they demonstrably land at 30 vs 35-55. The 'value is the minimum, not a ceiling' gloss (3424) concedes horn 2. The §6.6 koku-lockstep verifier (3393) is therefore failing or vacuous. NOVELTY: not a duplicate of round-1 Q1 (ceiling) or Q4 (runtime enforcement) — it is the REFUTATION of round-1's POSITIVE finding that praised 'rung-meter ≡ time-floor off one constant', showing one constant cannot yield both a flat-30 meter and §4.8.1's escalating 35-55 wall-clocks. DISPOSITION needs-human: fixing forces a design choice on locked surfaces — either make RUNG_FLOOR_MIN a per-rung escalating array (touches a LOCKED-direction constant; forces re-deriving the entire §4.1.1 threshold table) or declare the meter a 30-min-floor-only device with story-driven escalation (forces deleting the lockstep/land-together prose and conceding the §6.6 verifier does not check meter==wall-clock, re-opening Q4). Neither is a typo. Key locations: docs/prd.md §4.1.1 (3391-3416), §4.8.1 (4347-4360), §4.0 (3234-3235), master dials (4474-4477).

confirmed.title: Cross-pillar-combo 'excluded from the gate' is unrepresentable in GameState — and a static content-verifier cannot prove the runtime ∀-property
confirmed.where: §6.4 GameState influence shape (L5775-5777) + selector gateProfile (L5817) vs §4.3.1 rules 1-3 (L3763-3769) + §6.6.1 trade-post-combo proof (L5957-5959); build sites M5 phase 7 (L6808) and M6 phase 5 (L6870)
confirmed.detail: §6.4 stores `influence[pillar] = { value, highWater, dent, subEngines? }` — a SINGLE value per pillar, with no deed-vs-combo provenance split and no separate deed ledger (`counts` is explicitly NOT a deed store, L5786). §4.3.1 then demands three things that cannot coexist on that one field: rule 2 — a combo IS a real capped deed-jump that increments BOTH paired pillars' value (so it also raises highWater, which the seasonal 30% appraisal posts against); rule 3 — that same combo ip must be EXCLUDED when the hybrid gate evaluates 'good in ALL revealed pillars.' But `gateProfile(state)` is a pure selector over `influence[pillar].value`, which now contains the combo ip with no way to subtract it. Concretely: a T2 player could reach 'good in Arms' partly via Arms×Office + Arms×Name combos — exactly what rule 3 says is impossible — and it is the DEFAULT behaviour of the specified data model. Worse, §6.6/§6.6.1 assign the verifier to 'PROVE a combo can NEVER breach ⅓ nor satisfy a required pillar.' The content verifier is a STATIC pass over registries (id/ref resolution); a 'never across all reachable runtime states' quantified property is not something it can establish. It is reducible to static checks only if (a) the gate excludes combo-sourced ip [needs a field that does not exist] and (b) combos never write the trade sub-engine [never stated — Estate×Office/Estate×Name combos add to 'the Estate total' with the sub-engine unspecified]. As written, M5 phase 7 and the M6 verifier invariant are not implementable.
confirmed.fix: Decide the combo data model: either (i) add a per-pillar `gateEligibleValue` (deed-only) accumulator that combos do NOT write — gateProfile reads it, while `value`/highWater keep the combo for grind-feel; or (ii) keep combos out of `value` entirely (a display-only smoothing counter). Then state explicitly that combos target only the Estate land/treasury sub-engine (never trade) so the ⅓ proof becomes a static check on combo output targets. Which one?
confirmed.verdict:
REAL (core confirmed; framing refined). The claim reproduces from the text, but the root cause is sharper than stated: the PRD contradicts ITSELF on where a cross-pillar combo writes. §1.6.1 (L508), §2.16 (L2055-2059) and §4.3.1 (L3758/L3766/L3775-3776) say a combo emits "into BOTH pillars of the pair" — each side a capped deed-jump into that pillar's value (Model A, the broadened V2 canon D-031/Q22/FU20). But §6.5 content/influence.ts (L5884), the §6.6 verifier (L5908-5909) and the §6.6.1 trade proof (L5959) all say combos "land on a THIRD pillar (Name)" — the OLD single-Name-rebate that §4.3.1 explicitly says V2 broadened beyond. These cannot both hold.

Under Model B the §6.4 schema is fine and the static proof is clean: combos only touch the neutral, gate-excluded Name sink, so "combos write only Name; Name excluded from required-pillar checks" is a static structural invariant the content verifier CAN establish (which is exactly why §6.6 confidently claims "the verifier proves"). The human's broadening to BOTH-paired-pillars silently broke that — combo ip now lands on the very pillars the gate checks (Arms/Office/Estate) — and §6.4/§6.5/§6.6/§6.6.1 were never re-derived.

Confirming the representability gap under the broadened Model A: §6.4 (L5775-5777) stores influence[pillar]={value,highWater,dent,subEngines?} — one value, no deed-vs-combo provenance split; counts is explicitly NOT a deed store (L5786) and GameState (L5759-5791) has no deed ledger (log is capped), so a combo deed-jump summed into value/highWater is irreversibly destructive and un-subtractable. §6.4 gave the trade clamp dedicated 'real storage' (subEngines) but gave the gate-exclusion invariant NO equivalent — an asymmetry. §4.3.1 rule 3 (L3767-3769) + gateProfile (L5817) then demand exclusion with no field to do it; rule 2 also raises highWater, and the seasonal (30%) strand fires on new high-water marks (L527/L2074), so combo ip re-enters via seasonal too. Net: rule 3 and the M5 phase-7 (L6808) / M6 phase-5 (L6870) 'gate-excluded' verifier assertions have no structural hook as written.

Where the claim OVERSTATES: its second prong — 'a static verifier inherently cannot prove a runtime ∀-property' — is too broad. The PRD makes these invariants structural-by-construction (rule 1 post-clamp ordering is a static invariant, not a simulation), and the property IS statically provable under Model B; the real defect is the missing provenance field / the Model-A-vs-B contradiction, not static-verifier inadequacy. Also note the schema is additive-extensible at M5, so 'unrepresentable in GameState' is more precisely 'un-represented and unspecified.'

NOT in the round-1 ledger (distinct from B1 Name tie-out, the hybrid-gate-breadth-checked-too-late open Q, and the 'great/excellent counts-as-levers' trivial). NEEDS-HUMAN: resolving it forces a design call — either (1) revert §4.3.1 to the Name-sink model (clean/static-provable, no new field, but undoes the locked broadening D-031/Q22/FU20), or (2) keep the broadened model and add an additive-optional combo-provenance split AND decide the unresolved semantics of whether combo ip raises highWater / feeds the seasonal 30% strand / shows in the displayed total / reconciles with the §6.6.1 'accrual tie-out' (deed inventory includes combos via rule 2, but the gate excludes them via rule 3). The field-add is mechanical; the model choice and the highWater/seasonal interaction are balance-relevant design decisions, so a silent 'trivial fix' would risk locking an unreviewed default. PRD at /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md.

confirmed.title: M5 is the real 'secretly two (or three) milestones' — strictly larger than M3, and it introduces the cross-pillar-combo core system cold at its final phase
confirmed.where: §7.2 M5 'Lands' + 8 phases (L6755-6809); combo subsystem first built at M5 phase 7 (L6808); v1 content-complete gate hangs on it
confirmed.detail: Beyond the known M3-scope question (Q8), M5 packs MORE net-new systems into one slice than any other milestone: the G0-G7 ladder, the sekisho travel-standing gating layer (a brand-new gating primitive), the 3rd combat line + 4 weapons, the ENTIRE Origin faction (O0-O5 ladder + the double-gate STORY-AND-PILLAR predicate + the 'always ≥1 ungated beat so it never stalls' invariant + the missable O5 name-reclaim with a CONDITIONAL epilogue), the G6 Otsuru/Tama truth, the E3 'Prosperous' mega-build, the multi-stage Kuzuhara river-works LAND mega-lever, Hanzaki survived-not-won duels, belief-beast investigate-one-shots, rival-house eclipse, seasonal-reward rotation, AND — for the FIRST TIME anywhere in the build — the cross-pillar-combo subsystem plus its verifier 'proof' (phase 7). A foundational influence-accrual mechanic that the v1 end-gate depends on is being born in the last phase of the densest milestone, with no earlier milestone to de-risk it. This is the milestone most likely to stall the build mid-slice.
confirmed.fix: Pre-split M5 at the G3→G4 seam the way combat was pre-split M2a/M2b: M5a = G0-G3 climb + trade backbone + sekisho + Origin OPEN; M5b = G4-G7 + E3/Kuzuhara + payoffs + combos + end-gate. Better: move the cross-pillar-combo engine + its proof methodology to its own named slice so the new core mechanic lands before the content that leans on it. Agree to the split?
confirmed.verdict:
PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — but the claim's load-bearing thesis is REFUTED; a genuinely-new adjacent finding survives.

WHAT IS TRUE (factual core, reproduced from PRD):
- M5 has 8 phases (L6802-6809); M3 has 6 (L6704-6709), M4 has 7 (L6747-6753). So "strictly larger than M3" is TRUE by phase count. M5 is tied-largest (8) with M0 and M6.
- The cross-pillar-combo subsystem IS first built at M5 phase 7 (L6808) and nowhere earlier — grep confirms its first BUILD appearance is M5.7 (verifier proof then hardened at M6 phase 5, L6870).
- M5 does carry a brand-new sekisho travel-standing gating PRIMITIVE (built cold at phase 2, L6803) and the entire Origin faction subsystem (O0-O5 ladder + the double STORY-AND-PILLAR gate + the always-≥1-ungated-beat invariant + the missable O5 name-reclaim with conditional epilogue, phase 6 L6807). It is the v1-content-complete milestone and is NOT pre-split the way combat was (M2a/M2b split up front at the R3→R4 seam, L6485).

WHAT IS FALSE (the claim's headline thesis — REFUTED by §4.3.1):
- The combo is NOT "a foundational influence-accrual mechanic that the v1 end-gate depends on." §4.3.1 rule 3 (L3767-3769) makes combos EXPLICITLY gate-EXCLUDED: "combo-sourced ip is not counted — a combo can NEVER substitute for being good/great/excellent in a pillar on its own merits. It only smooths the grind feel, never the gate." The T2→T3 end-gate is satisfiable with ZERO combo ip: the §4.8.3 deed inventory (L4387: ~72 deeds summing to 70% of every good band) reaches the gate on its own; combos merely "smooth the grind."
- "No earlier milestone to de-risk it" is misleading: the combo is a small bounded rebate (≤ one 0.04 per-event cap split across a pair, §4.3.1 rules 2/4) that rides on machinery already built and verifier-checked at M3 — the four-pillar accrual engine, the trade-≤⅓ clamp, and the nested Estate subEngines{value,highWater} storage that exists precisely so combos compute post-clamp (L5777, M0/M3). The hard invariant (trade-never-breaches-⅓-via-combo) is a proof over de-risked storage, not a from-scratch foundational system.
- "v1 content-complete gate hangs on it" conflates "the milestone completes after its phase 7/8" (trivially true of any final phase) with a mechanical gate dependency (false). The combo is the OPPOSITE of foundational-to-the-gate: it is deliberately fenced out of it.
- Therefore the combo is NOT the thing "most likely to stall the build mid-slice" — it is among the smallest, most-fenced additions in M5.

THE GENUINELY-NEW FINDING THAT SURVIVES (round 1 missed this; it is distinct from Q8/M3-scope):
M5 — the milestone that declares v1 content-complete — is the largest and most subsystem-dense slice and has NO up-front split, yet round 1 only flagged M3 scope (Q8). M5 introduces THREE net-new subsystems (the sekisho gating primitive, the whole Origin faction with its novel double-gate + ungated-beat invariant + missable/conditional epilogue, and the cross-pillar combos), plus a wide content surface (G0-G7, 3rd combat line + 4 weapons, E3, Kuzuhara mega-lever, Hanzaki, belief one-shots, rival eclipse, G6 truth, seasonal rotation). The real stall risk is M5's BREADTH and lack of a pre-split — not the combo. This is a build-sequencing design call (a sibling to Q8): pre-split M5 the way M2a/M2b and the proposed M3 split work — e.g. at the G0→G3 climb / G4→G7 eclipse seam (already phases 4 vs 5), carving the Origin faction and the sekisho primitive into their own de-risked slices. Recommend logging as Q8b (M5-scope) alongside Q8.

DISPOSITION: needs-human — a milestone-split / sequencing decision, not a trivial text fix. The combo-specific claim should be marked refuted in the ledger (don't re-cut the combo as foundational); record the M5-breadth/no-pre-split concern as the new actionable item.

confirmed.title: Double-resurrection onto one mother (Oyuki) + over-repeated 'presumed-dead → back-from-the-dead' shape
confirmed.where: §5 T2.5 lines 5163-5171 (Jinpachi 'family presumed him dead'; Oyuki 'presumed him dead'); §3.6.2 O1 line 3004 (Oyuki) & O4 line 3007 (Jinpachi); §1.9 line 777; §1.3 line 188
confirmed.detail: Within the single short Origin reunion arc (O0→O5, G2→G6) the SAME mother, Oyuki, gets back two separately presumed-dead loved ones: her son (Tahei, O1) and her husband (Jinpachi, O4). Two independent 'thought-dead, actually alive, returns' to one grieving woman in one tier is a soap-opera contrivance. It compounds a structural over-use of the identical reveal-shape across the whole game: the PRD applies 'presumed-dead → back-from-the-dead' to the MC (§1.3/§1.9), to Otsuru (§1.9 'too ashamed to return'), AND to Jinpachi (T2.5 'parallels Otsuru + the kamikakushi master-lie'). By the third use the beat reads as formula, not resonance. The PRD's own §5 flag #2 (line 5505) gestures at this — 'whether his return re-reads Oyuki at all' — but only worries about debt-stacking, never the double-resurrection itself.
confirmed.fix: Decide whether Jinpachi must also be a presumed-dead return. Simplest: make Jinpachi merely absent/estranged (away on a long caravan circuit, or aged and ailing at home) rather than a second resurrection, so Oyuki's emotional payoff is the single return of her son. If both returns are kept, give Oyuki an explicit on-screen reckoning with surviving two 'deaths', and vary the third reveal's shape so it isn't a third 'alive-but-couldn't/wouldn't-come-home'.
confirmed.verdict:
CONFIRMED REAL (narrow form), genuinely new, not in the round-1 ledger (zero prior mentions). Reproducible from the text: within the single optional Origin arc (O0→O5, all inside T2), Oyuki gets back TWO separately-presumed-dead loved ones as separate, separately-paced beats — her son (MC) at O1 (§3.6.2 line 3004, "grieved-as-lost, now home") and her husband Jinpachi at O4 (line 3007), where O4 explicitly "opens LATE on the track," so it is NOT one homecoming wave but a second later payoff onto the same woman. Both are tagged presumed-dead (§5 lines 5163/5170; §1.7 line 734 "Grieved him as dead"). A literal double-resurrection onto one grieving woman in one short arc is verifiable.

NEW root-cause the claim and flag #2 both missed: the double-resurrection is a SIDE-EFFECT of the prior fix. The father was originally "Kuranosuke" with a debt-bondage arc (decisions.md 88-91); a verifier flagged debt-stacking; the Q11 reframe rewrote him as a clean "presumed-dead → back-from-the-dead" return — i.e. it SWAPPED debt-stacking for resurrection-stacking onto Oyuki. No doc names this.

Supporting (uncited) evidence: (1) the father's presumed-DEATH has NO authored CAUSE anywhere — the PRD authors his return repeatedly but never his disappearance, unlike the MC's flood/rockfall (§1.9 line 778); the only diegetic line ("They said the pass took you," 3007) is Jinpachi describing the MC's death, not his own. (2) Internal inconsistency = cheapest lever: §3.6.2 O4 says "grieved as AWAY/LOST" (alive-but-absent) while §5 T2.5 (5163/5166) says "presumed him dead / back-from-the-dead"; resolving toward O4's own "away/lost" erases the second resurrection with near-zero rewrite, but choosing that direction overrides the deliberate Q11 reframe — a design call, not a typo. (3) Pronoun slip at 5170 ("Oyuki … presumed HIM dead") reads as plausibly Jinpachi given the prior bullet — a symptom of loading two returnees onto one character.

CORRECTION to the claim: flag #2 (line 5505) is NOT only about debt-stacking — it explicitly asks "whether his return re-reads Oyuki at all," so the Oyuki load is half-seen; but it never NAMES it as a double-resurrection/redundancy, so this sharpens a half-open flag rather than surfacing a wholly-missed one.

PARTLY REFUTED — the broader "whole-game over-use makes the shape read as formula." The MC↔Otsuru pairing is defensible craft: framed as a deliberate mirror WITH inversion (Otsuru's return is partial — she may never forgive — the opposite of the MC's full homecoming), and kamikakushi IS the title-theme, so two contrasting uses read as intentional rhyme. The genuine soft spot is ONLY the Jinpachi/Oyuki instance: a literal repeat with NO inversion (a second complete warm reunion) onto the SAME recipient — that is where rhyme degrades into redundancy. The "third use reads as formula" claim holds for this one beat, not as a blanket over-use.

Disposition needs-human: it is a narrative tone/design decision (demote father to "away, known-alive" per O4's own wording / differentiate the two deaths / keep as-is), sitting squarely inside the existing [FLAG-HUMAN] #2 / Q11. New contribution for the human: the debt→resurrection reframe CREATED the Oyuki double-resurrection, the father's disappearance is causeless, and O4 vs §5 already disagree on "away/lost" vs "presumed dead."

confirmed.title: The gender-drift 'fair clue' is psychologically implausible and never actually seeded
confirmed.where: §1.4 lines 204-206 ('fixed male gender is load-bearing... a fair, re-readable clue'); §1.9 lines 766-768; §5 T2.2 beat 7 lines 5062-5064
confirmed.detail: The keystone mystery rests on the village collectively misremembering a real, local lost child's GENDER ('the legend remembering a boy' when Tama was a girl), and this misremembering is the design's stated load-bearing justification for the fixed-male protagonist. Two problems. (1) Plausibility: Tama vanished only 10 years ago, was old enough to 'run from a violent stepfather and a near-sale' (i.e. ~9-13, not a toddler), and people who knew her are alive in the village — Sayo (~16 now), 'a household that flinches at her name'. A whole village does not invert a known recent child's sex over a decade. (2) Fairness: the PRD calls it 'fair, re-readable', but the §1.9 clue list (sandal at the jizō, a flinching household, a debt-ledger mention, a post-town sighting, 'the legend remembering a boy') contains NO concrete in-world signal that Tama was a girl planted before the G6 reveal — the only gender datum the player gets is the legend's wrong 'boy'. So the clue only works backward, at the reveal, not forward. That undercuts both the mystery and the design rationale for having no character creator.
confirmed.fix: Seed at least one concrete, re-readable 'Tama was a girl' counter-signal before G6 (e.g. Obaa Kuni or Ryōa's register recording a girl's name; a kept girl's item; an old villager who slips and says 'she'), AND give an in-fiction reason the public legend degendered her (e.g. the family's shame over the near-sale made them bury her name/sex; the kamikakushi retelling abstracted 'a child' that wishful grief reshaped around the returned man). Otherwise soften §1.4's 'fair, re-readable' claim.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL — confirmed from PRD text, and round 1 did not flag it. The gender-drift "fair clue" is both internally contradictory (worse than the claim states) and mis-described as "fair." Cited text verified verbatim in /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md: §1.4 L204-206 ("fixed male gender is load-bearing… a fair, re-readable clue that only works if the protagonist is fixed-male"); §1.9 L765-768 (clue list + "the gender-drift is the fair clue"); §5 T2.2 beat 7 L5061-5064.

PRONG 1 (plausibility) — CONFIRMED, and I can sharpen it into a hard internal contradiction the claim only gestured at. The gender-drift exists to license the premise that the village accepts a young MAN (~18-20) as the returned lost child. But the two NAMED anchors of the legend and the clue-set are precisely the people who personally knew girl-Tama:
  • Sayo (roster L719) — "the headman's daughter, ~16; … the **living heart of the legend** — names him 'Tama' on sight." Her V0 ignition beat (L2751) is spontaneous, certain recognition: "A girl, Sayo, stops dead: 'Tama? You're alive?'" Tama vanished ten years ago (L177/762), so Sayo was ~6 then — old enough to durably remember a peer's sex. A person who knew girl-Tama cannot look at a grown man and spontaneously, certainly recognize him as the returned Tama. The keystone beat that ignites the entire legend is logically impossible if Sayo knew Tama — yet she is defined as the one who knew her best.
  • "a household that flinches at her name" (L765-766) is Tama's own family — the violent stepfather's household she ran FROM. They certainly know Tama was a girl (and likely that she ran). So at least one living household, plus Sayo, are immune to the "drift."
  Therefore the "village's legend has drifted to remember a boy" cannot be a genuine village-wide memory error: the very characters who carry/ignite the legend and supply the clues are the living witnesses who knew girl-Tama. The drift would have to skip exactly the people who anchor it. Coupling that makes it worse: Otsuru "**ran** from a violent stepfather and a near-sale into service" (L767-768, L740) — i.e. old enough (~9-13) to flee and be near-sold, "alive and grown… too ashamed to return." The same detail that makes her "she ran" backstory work (old enough to be widely known by sex) is the detail that breaks the gender-drift's plausibility. This is a coupled design knot, not a slip.

PRONG 2 (fairness/seeding) — SUBSTANTIALLY CONFIRMED with one caveat. The seeded clue list (sandal at the jizō; flinching household; "the legend remembering a boy"; debt-ledger mention; post-town sighting) plus the lone residual-ambiguity token (the weir-jizō unknown-hand offering, L649/4961) contains NO concrete forward signal that Tama was a girl. The only explicit gender datum the player receives pre-G6 is the legend's "boy" — which points toward the FALSE reading (the male MC, called Tama, IS Tama), since the player IS the boy the legend describes. So the clue actively misleads forward and only resolves backward at G6. CAVEAT: "re-readable" is defensible (it rewards rereading after the reveal); it is "**fair**" that is the overclaim — in mystery terms fair-play means forward-solvable from planted information, which this is not. (The debt-ledger "near-sale into service" and the post-town sighting of grown Otsuru COULD be authored to carry a feminine signal — selling a daughter into service is the gendered pattern — but the PRD never says they reveal gender, so as written no forward girl-signal is planted.)

NOT trivially fixable → needs-human. This is the keystone-mystery premise plus the no-character-creator design rationale, so it's an ADR-level call, and every plausible fix ripples. Options the human must choose among: (a) recast the "boy" drift as a *motivated* family cover-story (the stepfather's household put it about that the lost child was a boy to bury the near-sale shame) rather than organic amnesia — this also explains the flinch AND becomes a genuinely fair forward clue (a household insisting on the wrong gender is a solvable signal); (b) decouple Sayo's certainty — but she's defined as "the living heart of the legend," ~16, in a tiny village, so she'd have known Tama; changing this rewrites the main village quest-giver and the ignition beat; (c) age Tama down at disappearance — collides head-on with "old enough to run from a near-sale." It also requires deciding whether the village *literally believes* vs. *willfully projects* (Obaa Kuni already "knows it is coping," L720), and whether to plant an explicit forward girl-clue so "fair" earns its word. None of these is a word-swap. Recommend logging as a design question alongside the existing keystone-mystery items.

confirmed.title: Day-keyed weather/lunar/festival key omits season+year; and §2.2 vs §6.4/§2.19 contradict each other on whether season/year persist
confirmed.where: §6.7.1 L6008 deriveDayKeyed(seed,channel,day); §2.2 L1212-1216; §6.4 L5763; §2.19 L2229; §4 L3214 (30d/season, 120d/year)
confirmed.detail: deriveDayKeyed is keyed only on (seed, channel, day). What `day` means is self-contradictory across the doc. §2.2 L1212-1216 says 'only the day index and tick persist' — implying season/year are DERIVED from an ABSOLUTE day. But §6.4 L5763 and §2.19 L2229 both persist all four {tick, day, season, year}. The two horns each break something: (A) if `day` is absolute, then season=floor((day-1)/30)%4 and year=floor((day-1)/120) are fully derivable, so storing them violates the §6.4 'store only non-derivable' cardinal rule and lets stored season/year DESYNC from day after any migration/manual-edit/import. (B) if `day` resets per season/year (which the separately-stored season+year fields imply), then deriveDayKeyed(seed,'weather',5) returns the IDENTICAL value in spring/summer/autumn/winter of every year — weather/festival variation repeats on a 30- or 120-day loop, a non-living world. Separately, in BOTH horns, deriving 'lunar phase' from an RNG-style day hash is wrong: lunar phase is a continuous ~29.5-day cycle, not an independent per-day roll, so phases would jump randomly day-to-day instead of waxing/waning, and a 30-day reset can never represent a 29.5-day period.
confirmed.fix: Pin `day` as a single absolute monotonic day-count, make season/year DERIVED selectors (don't store them — fixes both the contradiction and the desync), key deriveDayKeyed on that absolute day, and define 'lunar' as an explicit cyclic phase = f(absoluteDay mod LUNAR_PERIOD) rather than an RNG hash. Decision needed: confirm absolute-day + drop stored season/year?
confirmed.verdict: CONFIRMED (genuinely new; absent from the round-1 lists and the audit ledger). The core contradiction is directly reproducible: §2.2 L1212-1216 defines WorldClock{tick,day,season,year} but states "only the day index and tick persist ... (only `day` does [serialise])" — i.e. season/year are DERIVED from an absolute day; yet §6.4 L5763 stores `clock:{tick,day,season,year}` and §2.19 L2229 persists `worldClock:{tick,day,season,year}` — all four. Under §2.2's absolute-day model, season=floor((day-1)/30) mod 4 and year=floor((day-1)/120) (§4 L3214: 30 d/season, 120 d/year) are pure functions of day, so storing them in §6.4/§2.19 violates §6.4's OWN cardinal rule at L5751 ("store only what is NOT derivable") and lets stored season/year desync from day after any migration/import/edit. deriveDayKeyed (L6008) is indeed keyed only on (seed,channel,day). The lunar concern is also real: the doc repeatedly buckets lunar with weather/festival "rolls" off "the day-keyed RNG sub-stream" (L5991, L1214, L1842-1843), and a per-day splitmix64 hash yields uncorrelated values, so the moon would teleport between phases instead of waxing/waning over a continuous ~29.5-day cycle — an anti-"living world"/anti-authenticity defect for an Edo game with lunar festivals. CAVEATS where the claim overreaches: (1) horn B (weather/festival repeating on a 30/120-day loop) is NOT the doc's stated design — §2.2 clearly intends an ABSOLUTE monotonic day under which weather varies across years; horn B is only a latent mis-implementation risk created by the storage contradiction, not a committed bug. (2) The lunar issue is flavor/immersion severity, not a balance break: no mechanical lunar hook exists anywhere — the §4.7.1 L4185 weatherMod formula uses only the 'weather' channel, and the only post-§2 lunar mention (L6011) is a generic "modifier". (3) The deriveDayKeyed signature technically permits a continuous lunar impl (return day mod ~29.5, ignoring seed), so randomness isn't forced by the signature alone — but the "rolls/RNG sub-stream" prose pushes the wrong impl, and a continuous cycle is impossible if day ever resets per season (30 != 29.5). Disposition needs-human (low-to-moderate severity): the storage fix has a clear correct target (persist only {tick,day} absolute; derive season/year) but is schema-affecting (touches §6.4 GameState interface, §2.19 shape, and M0 build steps §7.2/§7.3) and bundles a genuine design decision about what "lunar phase" is (continuous ephemeris vs random roll), which is player-facing world-sim behavior that should be locked/ADR'd per CLAUDE.md rather than silently edited.

confirmed.title: Multi-tab equal-saveCounter lost-update: two same-origin tabs silently clobber each other across the shared backends
confirmed.where: §6.8.1 L6089-6098 selectNewest (max saveCounter, savedAt tiebreaker); §2.19.1 L2276; no Web Locks/BroadcastChannel/storage-event anywhere
confirmed.detail: The newest-wins selector is a single scalar counter, not a vector clock, and there is no single-tab lock. Two tabs of the same origin (trivial on web/itch) both load counter=N, both progress independently, both autosave. IndexedDB and localStorage are SHARED across same-origin tabs, so each tab writes counter=N+1 with DIFFERENT state to the same shared backends. On the next load the counters TIE (both N+1); the savedAt tiebreaker resolves to whichever tab wrote last by milliseconds, silently destroying the other tab's entire session. The counter, being monotonic-per-context, can never detect the conflict — and the redundant 3-backend matrix gives false confidence while losing a whole tab's progress. Round-1 B3 was single-context (import-over vs autosave); this is concurrent cross-tab and unaddressed.
confirmed.fix: Add a single-active-tab leader election (Web Locks API or BroadcastChannel) so only one tab autosaves, OR a per-tab instanceId + storage-event listener that detects a foreign counter advance and prompts reload/merge instead of blind overwrite. At minimum, document multi-tab as unsupported and warn on a second tab.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL and genuinely new. Confirmed from PRD text: the newest-wins selector is a scalar monotonic saveCounter with a savedAt millisecond tiebreaker (§6.8.1 L6089-6098; §2.19.1 L2276 `selectNewest → max by saveCounter, then savedAtTimestamp`), and there is NO tab-coordination primitive anywhere in the 7000-line spec — grep returns NONE for navigator.locks/Web Locks, BroadcastChannel, the `storage` event/onstorage listener, and any multi-tab/concurrent/leader-election/mutex/single-tab language (the only "storage" hits are localStorage/sessionStorage backend mentions). saveCounter is an in-memory per-context increment ("increments once per successful save," L6091) with no read-modify-write against backends before incrementing.

The lost-update is reproducible: IndexedDB + localStorage are same-origin-shared, so two tabs that both load counter=N increment in-memory and write divergent state to the shared backends; last-writer-wins clobbers, and a scalar monotonic-per-context counter is provably unable to detect concurrent divergence (would require a vector clock, per-writer ID, or a lock — none present). The claim's "counters TIE → savedAt-by-ms picks the wrong session" is one concrete instance (symmetric first-autosave, where the reloading tab's own per-tab sessionStorage produces the tie); the underlying defect is broader (the higher-counter tab silently wins regardless).

Refutation attempts failed: (1) active-only/backgrounding-pauses (§6.9 L6173) does not mitigate — pausing the tick never re-reads storage on regaining focus, so a switched-to tab acts on stale in-memory GameState, and two side-by-side visible windows are both active at once; (2) the `app` magic field is identical across tabs; (3) sessionStorage being per-tab is what creates the tie, not a rescue. Distinct from Round-1 B3 (single-context import-over vs autosave) — this is concurrent cross-tab divergence, unaddressed.

Material new angle: the redundant 3-backend matrix is the PRD's headline save-robustness story (R3 risk L7002, M0 DoD item 5 L6547, "built in full at M0") yet provides zero protection against the most common real-world data-loss vector for a "leave it running for hours" idle game — a second tab — and manufactures false confidence.

Disposition needs-human: the fix is a missing mechanism plus a new player-facing UX state (Web Locks single-writer lock with 2nd tab read-only / "open elsewhere"; or BroadcastChannel leader election; or per-context writer-ID + on-load conflict-detection → recovery prompt). Choosing the multi-tab policy and what the second tab does is a design call, not a wording tweak — and it touches a save layer the PRD froze as built-in-full at M0.

confirmed.title: Multi-backend save 'redundancy' is non-independent against its OWN stated threat (itch cross-origin iframe), and the keystone test is deferred to the LAST milestone
confirmed.detail: The save layer's entire justification is 'survive itch's cross-origin-iframe partition/eviction' by writing redundantly to IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage, framed (L6031) as defence against 'any SINGLE backend may be wiped or isolated.' But the named threat is per-PARTITION, not per-backend: cross-origin-iframe storage partitioning/blocking hits IndexedDB and localStorage IDENTICALLY (same storage key), so they are not independent failure domains — the redundancy buys ~nothing against the actual risk. Two compounding facts the PRD never confronts: (1) sessionStorage is per-tab-session and dies on tab close, so as a 'durable redundant backend' it contributes ZERO persistence — effectively there are 2 durable backends, both in the same blocked partition. (2) On WebKit/Safari (one of the two M7 test targets), third-party (cross-site) iframe storage is blocked by default (ITP) absent a Storage Access API gesture — so on itch-in-Safari the 'transparent autosave' may simply never persist, leaving the manual base64 export ('the player's own safety net') as the ONLY real save, contradicting §2.19(b) 'autosaves transparently.' Worst of all: the layer is 'built FULL in M0' (committed first) but its survival is validated only at M7 (built last) — the load-bearing assumption is tested dead last, with no fallback plan if it fails. This is new structural evidence on a known-deferred item, not the deferral itself.
confirmed.fix: Human Q: given iframe storage partitioning hits all same-origin backends together (and WebKit blocks 3rd-party iframe storage by default), is cross-backend redundancy the right mitigation, or do we need (a) Storage Access API request on first gesture, (b) a top-level-window save fallback, and (c) treating base64 export as the primary durable save on itch? And: pull the cross-origin-iframe survival smoke EARLIER than M7 so the M0 architecture isn't validated last.
confirmed.verdict:
REAL — confirmed, with genuinely new structural evidence beyond round 1's "keystone-deferred-to-M7" note. This is a design/engineering gap, not trivially fixable; needs a human design call. Default was to refute, but the PRD text + actual web-platform storage semantics back the core of the claim.

WHAT IS SOLID (reproducible from PRD text + platform behavior):
1. Non-independence vs the NAMED threat. §6.8 L6031 frames redundancy as defence where "any SINGLE backend may be wiped or isolated," and §2.19.1(d) L2269 says "a backend may be wiped or read-only in the iframe sandbox." But storage partitioning applies ONE partition key (top-level-site + frame-origin) uniformly across IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage. Against the literally-named "partition" threat the three backends share one partition and are isolated/blocked together — they are NOT independent failure domains. The PRD's per-backend mental model is the wrong granularity and is never reconciled (grep: no "partition key" / "failure domain" / "independent"-in-storage-sense / "Storage Access" / "first-party" anywhere).
2. sessionStorage mis-counted as durable. §6.8/§2.19 list it as one of three co-equal "redundant backends," but it dies on tab close → zero cross-session durability. Effectively there are 2 durable backends, both in the same blocked partition.
3. The sharpest NEW point — silent ephemeral loss defeats the "no silent loss" guarantee. WebKit/Safari (a NAMED M7 target, M7 DoD L6899) blocks/ephemeralizes script-writable storage for cross-site iframes by default (ITP), and itch's HTML embed IS a cross-origin iframe (the PRD agrees throughout). An ephemeral write SUCCEEDS (and reads back within-session) then vanishes after the session — it does NOT reject. So Q44's "couldn't save — export a backup" banner (L6036, the whole "no silent loss" promise) never fires, and §2.19(b) L2217 "autosaves transparently" is silently false in the exact named environment; the base64 manual export (L6935 "the player's own safety net") becomes the ONLY real save. The PRD never confronts the write-succeeds-then-evicts case.
4. Keystone tested dead-last, no fallback. "FULL layer built in M0" (L6026/L6080) but survival validated only at M7 (L6910; R3 §7.4.1 "Caught at: M0 + M7"). No contingency is specified if the M7 test fails. Moreover the PRD's own M7 step-5 (L6910) uses a LOCAL cross-origin iframe via vite preview — so the core partition behaviour is locally testable at M0/M1; deferring the keystone-MECHANISM validation (vs only the itch-specific deploy smoke) to the last milestone is unjustified.

WHERE THE CLAIM OVERSTATES (adversarial honesty):
- "redundancy buys ~nothing" is too strong. The PRD's stated threat is "partition / eviction," and redundancy DOES still hedge per-backend availability/quota/throwing differences (e.g., IDB unavailable in some private modes while localStorage works; localStorage ~5MB quota fills independently). It buys little vs partition ISOLATION specifically, not nothing overall.
- sessionStorage isn't literally "zero persistence" — it survives a same-tab F5 reload (so the M7 "reload → autosave persists" smoke can pass on it deceptively), but gives zero cross-session durability. The over-count critique still stands.

WHY NEEDS-HUMAN (not a wording fix): the resolution requires real design decisions — (a) an actual durability strategy under partitioning (Storage Access API gesture, or a "open fullscreen / new tab" launch to reach a first-party context, or accept export-only on hostile embeds with a much more aggressive export nudge); (b) an ephemeral-write DETECTION mechanism (write-then-read-back canary across a session boundary) so silent eviction trips the "couldn't save" banner; (c) re-frame the rationale away from "any single backend" toward eviction/availability hedging + manual-export-as-primary on Safari-embed; (d) pull a LOCAL cross-origin-iframe partition test forward to M0/M1 and define an M7-failure fallback. These are roadmap + UX + spec changes, not a one-liner.

Note: distinct from known B3 (newest-wins/schemaVersion) and from the round-1 "background-tab throttling" / generic-deferral items — this is the redundancy's non-independence + silent-ephemeral-loss + premature-commit-late-validation structure, not the deferral per se.

confirmed.title: Multi-tab concurrent play silently clobbers a save — an unaddressed 'silent loss' vector distinct from B3
confirmed.where: §6.8 / §6.8.1 newest-wins selector; §2.19 — entire save section (no single-instance guard anywhere)
confirmed.detail: Grep across the whole PRD finds ZERO mention of multi-tab, BroadcastChannel, Web Locks (navigator.locks), the storage event, or single-instance enforcement. The newest-wins selector arbitrates across BACKENDS at load time but nothing arbitrates across two live TABS of the same game. Concrete failure: player has the game open in tab A (saveCounter 100); opens a 2nd tab B (loads counter-100 state); both autosave on their debounced/visibilitychange cadence, each independently incrementing to 101, 102… from their own divergent in-memory state. Whichever writes last wins by counter (timestamp tiebreaker on collision) and the OTHER tab's progress is silently overwritten — and the still-running tab's in-memory state now diverges from disk, so its next autosave re-clobbers. This is a routine real-world scenario (reopen the bookmark, restore-tabs on browser launch) and it directly violates the save layer's headline 'no silent loss.' It is a DIFFERENT vector than B3 (B3 = import-over vs higher-counter autosave; this = tab-A vs tab-B live writes), and neither is mitigated by the other.
confirmed.fix: Human Q: add a single-instance guard (Web Locks API to elect one writer, or a BroadcastChannel 'another tab is active' notice, or storage-event reconciliation), or explicitly accept last-tab-wins and warn the player when a 2nd tab opens?
confirmed.verdict: REAL — confirmed reproducible from the PRD; tried to refute and could not. (1) ABSENCE VERIFIED per-term across prd.md + prd_human_feedback.md + decisions.md: zero hits for multi-tab, BroadcastChannel, navigator.locks/Web Locks, the storage event/addEventListener, single-instance, instance, mutex, leader-elect, concurrent, "two/second/another tab". The only superficial hit, onstorage (16), is a false positive — substring of "sessi-onStorage"; the literal `storage` event and addEventListener are genuinely absent. Prior audit ledger: none. (2) The save model (§2.19/§2.19.1, §6.8/§6.8.1) arbitrates newest-wins ONLY across BACKENDS at load time, via a per-in-memory-instance monotonic saveCounter + timestamp tiebreaker (selectNewest = max by saveCounter, then savedAt). Two tabs share the SAME three backends (IDB+local+session) — nothing arbitrates two live writers. (3) Mechanism reproduces: autosave fires per-tab on a debounced cadence + visibilitychange/beforeunload (lines 6031-6033); load is one-shot ("load restores the exact saved GameState and resumes" — no re-read-on-focus), so tab B's in-memory state stays diverged from disk. Both tabs load at counter 100, each increments its OWN counter locally, both write to the shared backends → last-writer-wins; loser's progress silently overwritten, and the still-running tab re-clobbers on its next autosave. The headline "never silent loss" PROVABLY doesn't cover it: the only enumerated loss-guards are write-rejection (quota/private-mode/torn), foreign-app reject-to-recovery, and forward-schemaVersion fall-through — a concurrent overwrite is a VALID atomic put of a VALID same-app/same-version envelope, so the selector cannot even detect it. (4) Genuinely distinct from B3 (import-over vs higher-counter autosave + additive-field truncation): this is two live autosave streams; neither mitigation covers the other; per-instance counter monotonicity gives zero cross-tab protection. (5) FU23's "leave it running / tab-open auto-resolve+auto-repeat" (lines 948, 1318-1321) actively encourages leaving the game open in a tab — amplifying the forgotten-second-tab / restore-tabs / reopen-bookmark exposure. NEEDS-HUMAN: not a wording fix — requires a design call (Web Locks leader-election vs BroadcastChannel detect-and-pause vs detect-on-write reconcile), a second-tab UX (read-only / take-over / refuse), AND an itch cross-origin-iframe fallback (these APIs are origin-scoped and may be partitioned in the sandbox, the same constraint §6.8.1(d) already faces). ADR-worthy, parallel to D-013's multi-backend amendment. Locations: prd.md §2.19 (2204-2276), §6.8 (6022-6074; cadence 6031-6033; "no silent loss" 6036-6043), §6.8.1 (6078-6100).
